context:
Appendix A.2 ("Comonotonic quasi-convexity"), page 30 (PDF page 30/34 in parsed text)

One remaining question is whether a law-invariant cash-subadditive risk measure can be represented as the infimum of a set of law-invariant cash-subadditive risk measures with some other properties.
title: Lower-envelope representation of law-invariant cash-subadditive risk measures by law-invariant cash-subadditive risk measures with additional structure
latex:
Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,\mathbb P)\) be a nonatomic probability space and let \(\mathcal X=L^\infty(\Omega,\mathcal F,\mathbb P)\) be the space of essentially bounded real-valued random variables (identified \(\mathbb P\)-a.s.). A mapping \(\rho:\mathcal X\to\mathbb R\) is called a cash-subadditive risk measure if it satisfies:
- Monotonicity: if \(X,Y\in\mathcal X\) and \(X\le Y\) a.s., then \(\rho(X)\le \rho(Y)\).
- Cash subadditivity: for all \(X\in\mathcal X\) and all constants \(m>0\), \(\rho(X+m)\le \rho(X)+m\).
A risk measure \(\rho\) is law invariant if \(\rho(X)=\rho(Y)\) whenever \(X\) and \(Y\) have the same distribution under \(\mathbb P\).

Problem: Determine whether there exists a nontrivial property \(\mathcal P\) (strictly weaker than quasi-convexity, and possibly formulated in terms of comonotonicity or other dependence restrictions) such that the following representation holds:

Given any law-invariant cash-subadditive risk measure \(\rho:\mathcal X\to\mathbb R\), does there exist a family \(\mathcal C\) of law-invariant cash-subadditive risk measures each satisfying \(\mathcal P\) such that
\[
\rho(X)=\inf_{\psi\in\mathcal C}\psi(X)\quad\text{for all }X\in\mathcal X?
\]
Equivalently, characterize the largest class \(\mathcal P\) of additional structural properties for which every law-invariant cash-subadditive risk measure admits an infimum (lower-envelope) representation by law-invariant cash-subadditive risk measures satisfying \(\mathcal P\).

open_problem_quote: One remaining question is whether a law-invariant cash-subadditive risk measure can be represented as the infimum of a set of law-invariant cash-subadditive risk measures with some other properties.

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
